summary: Audience‑sourced tape with decent clarity but noticeable pitch issues captures a strong ’82 run featuring standout Cassidy/Loser and a memorable “I Need a Miracle” jam.
review: The show is widely praised as the best of the three‑night run, highlighted by tight performances of "Cassidy," "Loser," a powerful "Samson & Delilah" anchor and an unforgettable "I Need a Miracle" jam that leads into a soaring "The Other One." The recording is an audience mix of several sources – good enough to be called the nicest tape of this show, yet it suffers from pitch inconsistencies, variable image and occasional AUD chatter.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviewers consistently praise the musical performance (high‑quality setlist, standout jams) while noting mixed technical quality (pitch correction needed, uneven image). The strong show outweighs recording flaws, yielding a rating just above four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple audience sources stitched together; requires -2.5% to -3% pitch correction for most tracks, variable image and AUD chatter, missing drum fills into "Space" on some sections, but offers good clarity on the second set.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds momentum with "Cassidy" and "Loser," while the second set delivers a powerful "Samson & Delilah" anchor, a unique G‑jam within "I Need a Miracle," and a stormy rendition of "The Other One," making it the highlight of the run despite some average moments.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals are unusually forward; works hard on "Looks Like Rain" and matches Bob on "Loser".
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass rumble is noted just before the expansive "The Other One."
band_member_comments.Bob: Picked up a brief Crazy Fingers riff during the Miracle jam, showing attentive interplay.
band_member_comments.Drums: Missing drum fills into "Space" on some source tapes; overall drum presence varies across sources.
